title: After govt notice, Meta details child safety steps
content: Meta revealed new child safety measures after an Indian government notice regarding Instagram ads.  The company disabled numerous offending accounts and advertisements using advanced artificial intelligence tools.  Meta stated its systems detect suspicious off-platform links and other exploitation signals.  Globally, over four million accounts were removed last year for suspicious child-related activity.  The company continues to invest in technology and work with law enforcement agencies.

title: From 0-2 to 3-2: Messi's Argentina pull off impossible, storm into quarterfinals
content: Argentina staged one of the greatest comebacks of the 2026 FIFA World Cup, scoring three goals in the final 11 minutes to beat Egypt 3-2 and reach the quarter-finals. After falling 2-0 behind and seeing Lionel Messi miss a penalty, late strikes from Cristian Romero, Messi and Enzo Fernández completed a remarkable turnaround to keep the champions' title defence alive.

title: 2 pals built $13 mn AI startup by 21; say: Goal was not to build a million-dollar company
content: Two childhood friends launched an AI study app, generating over thirteen million dollars. Their initial goal was to create a useful product and fund college education. The Turbo AI app quickly gained traction after a viral TikTok video. They decided to leave college after their sophomore year to focus full-time. The founders caution against early college departures without proven business progress.

title: 'She's a nice person but made a mistake': Trump says ties with Meloni 'soured' over Iran war
content: US President Donald Trump described Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni as nice but criticized her stance.  He stated her refusal to support the US in the Iran conflict soured their relationship.  This dispute follows earlier claims by Trump about Meloni requesting a photograph.  The Italian government has decided to stop responding to Trump's provocative social media remarks.  Meloni's office also declined comment on their upcoming meeting at the NATO summit.

title: Virginia county manager: Electricity rates to rise sharply from July 1
content: As the tech landscape evolves with AI data centers, electricity costs are soaring. Henrico County, Virginia, has announced a staggering 25% hike in electricity bills for government facilities, prompting local officials to urge power conservation among their employees. Residents are also feeling the pinch, with some facing bill doubles. This phenomenon underscores the mounting pressure on community resources amid the rapid expansion of data centers.

title: Gujarat-bound Qatari LNG vessel with 4 Indian crew hit in suspected drone attack near Hormuz
content: A Qatar-bound LNG carrier carrying four Indian crew members was struck near the Strait of Hormuz. The vessel sustained damage and its engine room caught fire following the suspected drone attack. All 29 seafarers on board, including the four Indians, were reported safe after the incident. Qatar accused Iran of targeting the vessel, calling it a violation of international law.

title: Chennai tops domestic flights punctuality in May, Delhi & Mumbai at bottom: DGCA data
content: Chennai airport recorded the highest on-time domestic flight performance in May. IndiGo, Akasa, and Air India Group led airline punctuality among major airports. Domestic air travel saw over 1.5 crore passengers fly within the country last month. This represents a significant increase compared to the previous month's travel figures. Overall domestic air travel growth has remained tepid for the year.

title: World's largest asset management firm CEO is worried about how Mamdani is running NY
content: BlackRock CEO Larry Fink voiced concerns about New York City's business climate. He noted that 47% of city tax revenue comes from the top 1% of taxpayers. Fink stated that inadequate services do not justify the current tax burden for residents. He believes economic growth should be prioritized over tax policy by policymakers. Fink suggested that BlackRock might deploy US resources elsewhere if conditions weaken.

title: UK govt mulls emergency law to deport grooming gang ringleader to Pakistan
content: Labour is exploring emergency legislation to deport a grooming gang leader. Shabir Ahmed, convicted of child rape, was released after serving fourteen years. He is currently exempt from deportation due to an old immigration law. The government is considering legislative changes to address this loophole. Discussions are ongoing with Pakistan to facilitate his removal from the UK.

title: Woman gives birth to quadruplets in auto, all 4 newborns die
content: A woman gave birth to quadruplets in an auto-rickshaw while en route to a hospital. All four newborns tragically died shortly after their premature birth. The family alleges that an ambulance delay hindered timely medical care for the infants. Health officials stated the babies died due to prematurity and underdeveloped conditions. The mother is now out of danger and recovering at a health center.

title: Lionel Messi, FIFA face bias claims after Argentina's win over Egypt
content: Argentina’s 3-2 victory over Egypt generated debate far beyond the final score. Lionel Messi’s missed penalty, controversial decisions and Gianni Infantino’s reaction from the stands became major talking points online. While some fans claimed Argentina received favorable treatment, no evidence supports those allegations. On the pitch, the defending champions recovered from adversity and earned another place in the World Cup quarter-finals.

title: Travis Kelce's controversial tweets and Taylor Swift's high school nostalgia
content: Online discussions emerged following Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce’s wedding ceremony especially now when they are not posting any wedding pictures. Resurfaced tweets from Kelce have drawn criticism for their alleged fatphobic and homophobic content. Swift’s apparent obsession with high school experiences is also a topic of public conversation. This fascination stems from her being homeschooled during her later high school years.

title: Cowboys Trade Rumors: Dallas linked to Raiders' $100M star in blockbuster deal
content: The Dallas Cowboys have been linked to Raiders star Maxx Crosby in a proposed blockbuster trade that would send George Pickens and a first-round pick to Las Vegas. Former scout Bryan Broaddus believes the deal would strengthen Dallas' defense, while the Raiders would address their receiver needs. Crosby's recovery from knee surgery remains the biggest factor. Raiders insider Vincent Bonsignore said several teams are closely monitoring his health ahead of potential trade discussions.

title: Heavy rains trigger flash floods in Doda, damage houses and roads
content: Torrential rainfall caused flash floods and mudslides in Thathri town early Tuesday. Several houses were buried under debris, and many vehicles were damaged or washed away. The Doda-Kishtwar highway remains blocked due to landslides, suspending vehicular traffic. Restoration teams are working to clear debris and reopen the arterial road. Authorities are monitoring the situation and assessing the extent of the damage.

title: Before Delhi protest, NC secures Kashmir civil society’s resolution for statehood restoration
content: The National Conference is rallying support for its upcoming July 20 protest. Party leaders met with Kashmir civil society groups on Tuesday. These groups adopted a resolution demanding the restoration of J&K's statehood. The governing NC announced this one-day protest in Delhi earlier. This demonstration coincides with Parliament's monsoon session opening.

title: NHL Trade Rumors: Penguins' $30M Winger Linked to Canadiens Trade
content: Montreal's search for offensive help has naturally expanded into the trade market, bringing Rickard Rakell into the conversation after Elliotte Friedman's latest report. The veteran winger remains productive and carries a team-friendly contract, making him an appealing option on paper. However, his age, Pittsburgh's expected asking price, Montreal's long-term roster strategy, and Rakell's limited trade protection all complicate negotiations. At this stage, the connection appears to reflect routine due diligence rather than an indication that a deal is imminent, with other win-now contenders arguably better positioned to complete a trade.

title: J&K HC rejects Cong worker’s plea to shift trial from Srinagar to Jammu
content: The High Court of Jammu and Kashmir and Ladakh rejected a Congress worker's plea. He sought to shift his cheque bounce trial from Srinagar to Jammu. The court stated terrorism is almost eliminated in the Valley now. It noted the petitioner's claims of threat were improbable and lacked merit. The court found the petition was filed to avoid payment and prolong proceedings.

title: 'Op Hard Ball': FBI targets Bishnoi network; 50+ raids across US, Canada & Europe
content: In a coordinated effort, federal agents executed extensive raids in both the United States and Europe, leading to the detention of approximately twenty individuals. Authorities successfully seized an array of contraband, numerous firearms, and a large sum of cash believed to be illicit. This operation specifically focused on eliminating crime factions allegedly operated by Jaggu Bhagwanpuria and Goldy Brar, with the goal of disrupting their international crime network.

title: Horoscope today: Daily astrological predictions for July 8, 2026
content: Horoscope Today: Read daily horoscope predictions for July 8, 2026. Know all about the astrological events and influences that will be affecting each of the 12 zodiac signs. Our astrologer has analyzed the movements of the planets and the alignment of the stars to bring you the most accurate and up-to-date horoscope predictions for the day ahead. Get ready to discover what the stars have in store for you today.

title: 1,700-year-old inscription may show how Christianity overtook Roman cult
content: An Aramaic inscription found at Zerzevan Castle documents the closure of a Mithras temple. This ancient text provides rare written evidence of religious transition in Roman Turkey. The inscription, dating to the third or fourth century AD, mentions both Mithras and Jesus Christ. It also includes references to the Holy Cross, signifying a Christian presence. This discovery adds to other early Christian archaeological finds in present-day Turkey.

title: Katherine Heigl to sell 25-acre Utah mountain home as family starts new chapter
content: Actress Katherine Heigl and musician Josh Kelley are selling their sprawling 25-acre Utah mountain estate for $10.6 million after nearly two decades. The custom-built home, a hub for their family life and creative pursuits, is being left behind as they embrace a new chapter in a smaller, nearby Victorian farmhouse.

title: Ranking Taylor Swift's best songs inspired by her famous exes
content: Taylor Swift’s extensive list of former romantic partners fuels her songwriting. Fans connect deeply with her authentic and heartfelt lyrical narratives. In this article, we rank her songs based on their perceived connection to specific muses. The ranking highlights relationships with figures like Joe Jonas, Jake Gyllenhaal, Joe Alywn, Taylor Lautner, and more. Swift’s ability to transform personal experiences into music resonates widely because it feels personal at every level.

title: Footballer Nolan Xavier Wells found dead after Fourth of July disappearance
content: The body of missing footballer Nolan Wells was found off Horn Island. He vanished during Fourth of July celebrations after a boat trip with friends. Search teams combed the island and surrounding waters for the missing teenager. His family is heartbroken and seeking privacy during their grief. Authorities are investigating the cause of his untimely death.

title: Indian woman in US shows her Rs 4 crore Connecticut middle-class home
content: An Indian woman living in the United States has gone viral after sharing a video tour of her Connecticut home, offering viewers a glimpse of what she describes as a "middle-class family home" in America. Her video has sparked conversations on social media, with many comparing the size and pricing of suburban homes in the US with properties in India.

title: 'What was Sonam Raghuvanshi asked?' Police find chilling Google searches on Siya Goyal's phone
content: The investigation into Ketan Agrawal's murder has led Pune police to scrutinize Siya Goyal's internet activities for potential evidence. Notably, her search history includes queries tied to an ongoing conspiracy case involving Sonam Raghuvanshi. Authorities are also looking into allegations that Goyal and her supposed partner secretly wed ahead of the incident.

title: Messi makes unwanted record with penalty miss, becomes first player to ...
content: The Argentina captain failed from the spot during his team's round-of-16 match against Egypt in Atlanta.

title: 'Like to sing & dance to Indian songs': Indonesian president's 'DNA' remark draws praise from PM Modi
content: Indonesian President Prabowo Subianto expressed admiration for Prime Minister Narendra Modi's governance. He acknowledged studying and adapting Indian policies for Indonesia's development. Subianto also praised India's democratic system and election process. Indonesia closely studies India's peaceful transfer of power mechanisms. These remarks were made during Prime Minister Modi's recent three-nation visit.

title: Novak Djokovic reunites with Wimbledon ball girl, gifts her signed cap
content: Novak Djokovic shared another memorable moment at Wimbledon after reuniting with the ball girl who became part of his viral prank during the second round. The Serbian star met the youngster ahead of his quarter-final match against Felix Auger-Aliassime and turned the light-hearted incident into a heartwarming meeting. Their friendly conversation and warm exchange quickly caught the attention of tennis fans.

title: Delhi-Jaipur highway cave-in near Gurgaon triggers 10-km traffic jam, advisory issued
content: Gurgaon experienced its first heavy monsoon rain, causing significant disruptions. Road cave-ins and severe waterlogging affected key arterial roads across the city. Thousands of commuters were stranded as traffic jams stretched for kilometers. Authorities issued advisories and implemented route diversions to manage congestion. Efforts are underway to clear waterlogged areas and repair damaged infrastructure.

title: The mystery of 8: Why PM Modi mentioned this number in his Indonesia speech
content: Prime Minister Modi emphasized the number eight during his address to the Indian diaspora in Jakarta.  He noted numerical connections between India's Republic Day and President Prabowo's birthday.  The number eight holds significant cultural meaning in Indonesia, symbolizing prosperity and good fortune.  This visit saw twenty agreements signed, strengthening bilateral cooperation between the nations.  India will also assist in conserving the ancient Prambanan Temple, a shared heritage site.

title: India strengthens position as investment destination; FDI rises by 44% to $39 billion in 2025
content: South Asia witnessed a sharp rise in FDI inflows, which climbed from $34 billion to $46 billion, largely driven by India, where inflows expanded to $39 billion, the report noted. At the same time, UNCTAD observed that project-related indicators suggested a more cautious investment environment despite the increase in overall inflows.

title: Badrinath donation row: BKTC suspends employee as probe into alleged theft begins
content: An employee was suspended as an inquiry panel investigates donation handling irregularities.  The probe began after social media allegations surfaced regarding offerings at Badrinath Dham.  CCTV footage and employee statements will be examined by the four-member committee.  Questions arose about CCTV camera replacements, which officials stated was routine.  The controversy has also sparked political debate and calls for further investigation.

title: Delhi-NCR office leasing hits record high; flexible operators account for 45%
content: Flexible workspace operators are now leading Delhi-NCR's office leasing activity. This trend reflects a growing preference for agile and adaptable office portfolios. Companies are increasingly adopting a "core plus flex" model for their workspace needs. India's overall office market also saw record absorption during the quarter. Demand remains strong for high-quality, sustainable, and flexible office assets.

title: 'Security always concern': BCB ready to enhance safety for India's potential tour
content: Bangladesh Cricket Board assures top-level security for India's upcoming tour.  The board is prepared to further strengthen its arrangements if required.  Formal discussions with the BCCI are yet to begin, but confidence remains high.  Recent invitations for future fixtures signal growing optimism for the series.  Security remains a top priority for the BCB during the planned visit.

title: Baruipur rape-murder case: West Bengal Police intensify probe; examine shack as possible crime scene
content: “Three arrested accused were being questioned even as investigators were awaiting the forensic and viscera examination reports,” says senior police officer

title: Record 1.3 lakh pilgrims visit Amarnath shrine in 5 days in Kashmir
content: Our goal is to make the pilgrimage a truly memorable experience for everyone and they have access to the best possible amenities, says Lieutenant Governor Manoj Sinha

title: Ahead of Delhi protest, NC leaders meet civil society, pass resolution on Statehood for J&K
content: National Conference leaders term the delay in restoration of Statehood as ‘inordinate’ and describe the Centre’s ‘appropriate time phrase’ as a ‘vague and timeless expression’

title: Chief Minister Vijay “blackmailed” children by appealing to them, two election petitioners allege before Madras High Court
content: Justice V. Lakshminarayanan orders notices on the election petitions filed by DMK’s R.D. Shekar and S. Inigo Irudayaraj who had lost in Perambur and Tiruchi East constituencies

title: Staff stole cash, security protocols not enforced: Ram Temple SIT report
content: CCTV footage shows some counting staff hiding currency notes in clothes, pockets, and shoes; SIT identified procedural flaws in management of donations, to conduct re-audit of accounts

title: Decision over alliance with TVK yet to be reached, says Thirumavalavan
content: On July 1, the Chief Minister spoke to the parties that extended support, but no final decision was taken, he clarifies

title: Arcot MLA clarifies his absence at AIADMK consultative meeting
content: The Arcot MLA said that he had informed the party leadership about his inability to attend the consultative meeting at the AIADMK’s headquarters in Chennai

title: Cracks wide open in Punjab Congress as Channi camp skips Baghel’s outreach meetings
content: Punjab in-charge meets District Congress Committee chiefs, senior leaders as part of preparations for Assembly election; Warring downplays crisis, says Channi to meet Baghel within a day or two

title: 2008 Ahmedabad serial blasts: High Court upholds death penalty for 38; government calls verdict a historic victory
content: On July 26, 2008, a series of 21 bomb blasts ripped through Ahmedabad in a span of 70 minutes, killing 56 persons and injuring 200 others

title: Gang held for possession of ganja near Katpadi
content: The police said that as part of surveillance, they were patrolling the check post near Katpadi when the team intercepted the gang

title: CM invites KCR for debate on Kaleshwaram lapses in Assembly or joint sitting of Legislature
content: Revanth Reddy assures steps to see that KCR is not belittled; dates and duration of the session to be of the former Chief Minister’s choice

title: The little songbooks that chronicled a historic temple entry
content: Songbooks were published both in support of, and in opposition to, people belonging to the oppressed castes entering the Madurai Meenakshi Amman Temple for the first time, over 87 years ago

title: Panels will be set up for better coordination between party and Karnataka govt.: KPCC
content: Karnataka Pradesh Congress Committee president B.K. Hariprasad said that for greater coordination between the State government and the party, a political committee and an implementation committee will be set up

title: Karnataka High Court questions why MLA M.C Sudhakar is not made accused in alleged land grab case against his father Chowdareddy, former Home Minister
content: The High Court of Karnataka has questioned why Chintamani MLA and former Higher Education Minister M.C. Sudhakar and his brother, M.C. Balaji, were not arraigned as accused in an alleged land grab case against their father, former Home Minister Chowdareddy, despite being named as direct beneficiaries of the encroached government land in the complaint

title: Karnataka Minister Khandre accuses Union government of selling HMT land for real estate businesses
content: Hitting back at Union Minister for Heavy Industries and Steel H.D. Kumaraswamy on the HMT forest land issue, Rural Development and Panchayat Raj Minister Eshwar Khandre on Tuessday accused the Centre of selling forest land illegally for real estate businesses

title: Online registration of select documents to be launched in Tamil Nadu on August 17: Minister
content: The documents include first sale of plots (by plot promoters) and the first sale of flats (by builders).

title: Senthilbalaji seeks anticipatory bail in ₹35-crore MLA bribery case
content: His plea is expected to be listed for hearing before Justice G.K. Ilanthiraiyan of Madras High Court on Wednesday

title: India supports two-state solution for solving Israel-Palestine conflict, says PM Modi
content: In a press statement in Jakarta while presiding over the signing of multiple agreements including the delivery of BrahMos supersonic cruise missile system and Astra Mk-1 beyond the visual-range air-to-air missiles to Indonesia, Mr. Modi says India believes that the role of dialogue and diplomacy has become more important than ever before

title: Wayanad debris slip updates: At least three dead, five others missing; region braces for more rains
content: The government has shifted local residents to safer locations. It has also halted traffic on the Meenakshi bridge and ordered the PWD to review its structural safety

title: Scientists develop health tracker for high-altitude yaks
content: The Internet of Things-based device is designed to address major challenges of yak management in remote regions and keep tabs on their movement near international borders in the Himalayas

title: ‘Flagging of labour rule violations could have led to CorroHealth lay-offs in Kozhikode’
content: The laid off staff are pinning their hopes on the talks scheduled between government officials and management representatives in Kochi on July 10

title: Monsoon tracker highlights: Wayanad on red alert after three dead in debris slip; Mumbai gets respite after two days
content: The alert was issued at 12.30 p.m. after the landslip at the Kalladi tunnel project site in Wayanad at around 11 a.m. Meanwhile, officials said ten people had died in rain-related incidents across Maharashtra's Palghar since July 1

title: Now, eggs are hurled at CPI(M) leader Minakshi Mukherjee
content: Since they were voted out of power on May 4, Trinamool leaders, including general secretary Abhishek Banerjee, MP Mahua Moitra, MLAs Madan Mitra and Kunal Ghosh, and several others, have had eggs hurled; this is the first such incident against a CPI(M) leader

title: BRICS nations adopt Guwahati Declaration to combat drug trafficking
content: India calls for a partnership built on speed, trust, and seamless real-time intelligence sharing to fight transnational drug syndicates

title: Four States reach agreement on Narmada project
content: Under the pact, the payments to be made towards the final settlement of pending dues have been resolved through a one-time settlement

title: 55% teachers are women, girl student enrolment up: govt.
content: UDISE+ report shows falling dropout rates and higher student retention; more schools get access to computers and internet; number of teachers up by 8.3% compared to 2022-23; learner progression across stages grows stronger

title: Karnataka SIR: Enumeration form distribution crosses 77% in Karnataka
content: Among districts, Belagavi recorded the highest number of enumeration forms distributed at 30.9 lakh, followed by Mysuru (22.27 lakh), Kalaburagi (19.07 lakh), Tumakuru (19.39 lakh) and Bengaluru Urban (16.9 lakh)

title: Houses, shops hit by mudslides in J&K’s Doda after cloudburst, torrential rains
content: Houses, shops, vehicles submerged under debris in Thathri town; Doda-Kishtwar national highway closed at several points; J&K L-G says no casualties, directs district administration to speed up relief and restoration work

title: Independence in legal profession just as crucial as judicial independence, says Supreme Court judgment
content: Apex court directs BCI to hold performance audit; proposes National Legal Academy on the same lines as National Judicial Academy for continued legal education of advocates

title: Home Minister stresses proactive steps to mitigate El Niño impact
content: Anitha directs officials to strengthen preparedness as Andhra Pradesh braces for below-normal monsoon rainfall

title: Punjab Congress crisis: Dissident leaders camp in Delhi as Baghel holds meetings to steady ship
content: The overhaul of the Punjab Congress has sparked an internal turmoil; Charanjit Singh Channi has held meetings intended to convey his displeasure to the party high command

title: High-level probe ordered into alleged irregularities with donations at Badrinath shrine
content: Shri Badrinath-Kedarnath Temple Committee suspends the personal assistant attached to the office of the committee’s chairman

title: ED arrests former CFO of Apollo Green Energy Ltd.
content: Gupta was arrested after failing to comply with four summonses and despite the issuance of a non-bailable warrant by a Gurugram court

title: Tamil Nadu drew lower quantity of rice than its share under Antyodaya Anna  Yojana in five of the last seven years
content: From 2019-20 to 2025-26, it was during the COVID-19 pandemic year (2020-21) that the drawal was almost close to the allocated figure of around 6.99 lakh tonnes, with the off-take being about 98%. In the remaining four years, it varied from around 73% during 2022-23 and 2023-24 to 79% during 2024-25

title: Modi announces IIM Bangalore campus in Indonesia
content: PM describes the initiative as a major step towards strengthening educational ties and benefiting students across the ASEAN region

title: US Revokes Iran Oil Waiver After Tanker Attacks In Strait Of Hormuz
content: The US Treasury Department canceled a license announced in June that had allowed Iran to produce, sell and deliver crude oil and related products through August 21.

title: Global Crackdown On Bishnoi Gang: 24 Arrested, Assassination Plot Busted
content: According to federal prosecutors, Bishnoi allegedly directed an international criminal network from inside prison using contraband mobile phones and encrypted internet communication devices.

title: French Far-Right Leader Le Pen Says She Will Run For President Next Year
content: Born in 1968, Le Pen was just four years old when her father Jean-Marie co-founded the National Front (FN), France's main postwar far-right movement.

title: Rape-Murder Of 11-Year-Old In Bengal Pre-Planned: Police After Three Arrests
content: At the crime scene, NDTV found that to avoid public attention, the criminals had chosen a deserted area adjacent to a railway track near the Suryapur station.

title: Air India, AI Express Flights Come Face-To-Face On Mumbai Runway
content: The incident took place at around 10 pm. The Air India Express plane was yet to vacate the runway after landing when the Delhi-bound Air India aircraft was preparing to take off from the same runway, the sources said.

title: After Setback, DMK Approaches Top Court Panel, CBI Ahead Of Vijay's Karur Visit
content: The move came just hours after the Supreme Court questioned the party's plea seeking curbs on Vijay's speeches and public engagements in connection with the September 2025 Karur stampede case, prompting the DMK to withdraw its petition.

title: Uddhav Thackeray Asks Party Officials To Take Ram Temple Campaign To District Level
content: On Sunday, Thackeray launched the Ram Raksha Andolan against the alleged donation theft at the Ram temple in Ayodhya.

title: Heavy Rainfall Triggers Rockfall In Uttarakhand, 32 Roads Closed
content: Most parts of Uttarakhand received moderate to heavy rainfall on Tuesday, with falling debris from the hills leading to closure of 32 roads, officials said.

title: Trump Praises "Chemistry" With Turkish President, Bashes NATO At Summit
content: Trump was greeted personally by Erdogan on the tarmac as he descended from Air Force One, before being escorted through the empty streets of the Turkish capital by a guard of riders on white horses.

title: PMO Holds Meeting To Review El Nino Impact On Agriculture, Economy
content: Secretary, Agriculture made a detailed presentation on the preparedness for possible impact of El Nino during the kharif season.

title: Mumbai Flooding 'Our Own Creation', Stop Blaming Civic Body: High Court
content: Expressing sharp displeasure, Acting Chief Justice Ghuge noted that even the footpaths outside the High Court are occupied by illegal shops.

title: On Camera, Israeli Officer Tosses Stun Grenade Into Car Carrying Palestinians
content: The CCTV clip, recorded on Sunday and released by Israeli human rights group B'Tselem, shows an officer confronting a vehicle before the situation quickly escalates.

title: Under-Construction Manhattan Skyscraper's Columns Buckle, Hundreds Evacuated
content: The 1970s-era office building was being converted to luxury apartments, and is the former global headquarters of pharmaceutical giant Pfizer.

title: In PM Modi's Speech At Indonesian Parliament, A Nehru Mention
content: "Both our countries have made diversity the very foundation of our unity within our democracies," Prime Minister Narendra Modi said in the Indonesian parliament

title: 8-Km Jam, Road Cave-In: Heavy Rain Takes Weighty Toll On Gurugram
content: A huge road cave-in on NH-48 left several vehicles stranded from hours, causing traffic to crawl from Rajiv Chowk in Gurugram.

title: 3 Tankers, Including India-Bound Vessel, Attacked In Hormuz In 24 Hours
content: One of the tankers caught fire after being attacked while travelling off the coast of Oman. Although theIranian state television said the liquefied natural gas tanker came under attack after ignoring warnings, it did not directly claim the assault.

title: After Accidents, Goa To Halt New Licences For 'High-Risk' Rental Thar SUVs
content: State Transport Minister Mauvin Godinho revealed the government's intention after holding a high-level meeting with Transport Department officials.

title: India Calls for Human-Centric, Inclusive AI Governance at UN Global Dialogue
content: Minister of State for External Affairs Kirti Vardhan Singh said AI governance must be rooted in meaningful human oversight, respect for human rights and international cooperation.

title: 'No Political Interference': Cops On Guwahati Road Rage Investigation
content: During the probe, investigators traced the Thar SUV seen in the viral video to its owner, Abhilash Dutta.

title: Trump admin eyes tighter H-1B, green card rules; Indians could be most affected
content: Lubna Kably is a senior editor, who focuses on various policies and legislation. In particular, she writes extensively on immigration and tax policies. The Indian diaspora is the largest in the world; through her articles she demystifies the immigration-policy related developments in select countries for outbound students, job aspirants and employees. She also analyses the impact of Income-tax and GST related developments for individuals and business entities. Read More

title: 6 accused hired on referrals from trust-linked people: SIT on Ram Temple donation 'theft'
content: When not covering crime, he reads fiction, roams around the dark alleys of the city in the dead of night. An MA (English Litt) from University of Lucknow, Pathikrit loves to speak French, well at least a smattering of it. He did his graduation in French language. A recipient of the Road Safety Fellowship of the United Nations, Pathikrit drives cautiously and advises others to do that too. Read More

title: Landslides disrupt Maharashtra traffic, Mumbai-Pune rail services remain off track
content: Neha Madaan is a senior feature writer at The Times of India, Pune. She holds an M A degree in Mass Communication and Journalism from University of Pune. She covers tourism, heritage development and its conservation, apart from an array of subjects such as civic issues, environment, astronomy, civic school education as well as social issues concerning persons with disabilities. Her interests include metaphysical research and animal rights. Read More

title: Shiv Sena neta ‘attacks hospital staff’, Kalyan-Dombivli civic body's doctors, nurses go on strike
content: patients seeking OPD consultations and routine treatment faced inconvenience . doctors, nurses and paramedical staff went on a strike to protest the assault of a hospital’s staffers . the protesters said their agitation will continue until the accused, including the corporator, are arrested .
